prose:
Method provenance (frontier orbital gap):
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference) in eV.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) with density fitting, as implemented in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry supplied by the user (/home/erobles/…/formaldehyde.xyz); no additional relaxation (single-point calculation at DFT level on that geometry, i.e. DFT//input-xyz).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software/backend: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF engine (SCF converged in 8 cycles).
SCF / convergence: scf_converged = true, 8 cycles; integrity checks all passed (status OK, trustworthy = true). No warnings reported by the engine.
Live log: calculation was executed via
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp2nb6ui2d/live.json
Stdout/stderr were streamed to the automatically generated .out file in the current working directory and could be inspected live during the run.

Result:
HOMO = −7.6523067 eV
LUMO = −1.7826355 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV (gas phase, B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input geometry)

No engine warnings were reported.
